meta.setting: Trail junction and camp edge outside French Gulch
meta.rdr_clone_target: PS3-era RDR-clone western art, grounded in practical marks, trail warnings, and camp work
meta.issue_title: Image: Brands, Blazes, and Camp Calls

research_grounding.title: NPS Whiskeytown History and Culture
research_grounding.use: Roads, work camps, ranching, mining, and regional settlement context.

research_grounding.title: NPS The Wintu
research_grounding.use: Native community context used to avoid invented or appropriated sign systems.

image_prompt.subject: a close view of practical western trail marks: a cattle brand burned into a rail, axe blazes on pine, boot-scraped arrows, stacked stones, and a camp-call hand gesture near a fire
image_prompt.scene: late dusk at a trail junction; saddles nearby, low coals, dust on boots, no readable writing, and marks visible as work language rather than secret code
image_prompt.composition: square still-life-plus-landscape with marks arranged naturally across wood, stone, dirt, and firelight; a rider's hands demonstrate a plain camp call
image_prompt.camera: close inspection angle like a game clue plate, with shallow camp background and readable materials
material_cues: river stones in a small cairn
image_prompt.hand_cut_note: Make the marks feel improvised by tired hands, each one useful and a little suspicious.

style_modifiers.medium: PS3-era open-world western game art, early-HD in-engine clue still life
render_traits: slightly simplified hands, rail, bark, stones, and saddle shapes
modest texture resolution on bark, dust, leather, ash, and burned wood
hard ambient occlusion under stones, rails, hands, and saddle straps
dusk firelight with cool trail shadow
practical clue composition rather than decorative symbol sheet
style_modifiers.palette: campfire orange, pine bark brown, ash gray, dust tan, saddle leather dark brown, brand black, stone blue-gray, and dusk violet.

text_rendering.instructions: No readable letters, words, alphabets, labels, brands as text, or invented Native marks. All marks must be non-text practical shapes.

negative_prompt: copy of commercial game characters, missions, towns, or logos

self_check: Reads as PS3-era western game art at square page-card scale.
The brand scar, blazes, boot scrape, cairn, hands, and camp edge are readable.
Every visible prop belongs plausibly to 1899 trail and camp life.
The marks are practical and not a fake sacred or secret symbol system.
No copied game characters, readable modern text, or inherited non-western symbols.
